package service

import (
	"fmt"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Positive scenario for service_method_accepts_map_string_any_input: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Services that use map-shaped dynamic input instead of typed request contracts.
type CaseServiceMethodAcceptsMapStringAnyInputModel struct {
	ID       string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
	TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
	Status   string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
	Amount   int    `gorm:"column:amount" json:"amount"`
}

type CaseServiceMethodAcceptsMapStringAnyInputRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

type CaseServiceMethodAcceptsMapStringAnyInputService struct {
	repo *CaseServiceMethodAcceptsMapStringAnyInputRepository
}

func (s *CaseServiceMethodAcceptsMapStringAnyInputService) Process(input map[string]any) error {
	id, ok := input["id"].(string)
	if !ok || id == "" {
		return fmt.Errorf("missing required field: id")
	}

	tenantID, _ := input["tenant_id"].(string)
	status, _ := input["status"].(string)
	amount, _ := input["amount"].(float64)

	model := CaseServiceMethodAcceptsMapStringAnyInputModel{
		ID:       id,
		TenantID: tenantID,
		Status:   status,
		Amount:   int(amount),
	}

	if err := s.repo.db.Create(&model).Error; err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create record: %w", err)
	}

	tags, _ := input["tags"].([]any)
	for _, tag := range tags {
		tagStr, _ := tag.(string)
		_ = tagStr
	}

	return nil
}
